architecture_gap: Recursive evolution requires persistent event history, swarm isolation, telemetry, and deterministic recovery. None of those existed operationally.
data_model_changes: Add event logs, knowledge graph edges for proposals/tasks/councils, and recovery manifests that point to deterministic replay sources.
governance_implications: Governance evolves from prose rules into executable review surfaces while still preserving human override.
invention_concept: Promote AAS into an event-sourced recursive control plane where swarms, councils, proposals, and recovery are first-class runtime entities.
migration_strategy: Begin with local JSONL/event-store files and expand toward service-backed components only after the file-backed control plane proves stable.
problem_statement: The current AAS can design Atrahasis artifacts, but it cannot recursively redesign itself as a continuously improving architecture evolution engine.
proposed_architecture_change: Adopt event-sourced telemetry logs as the runtime backbone, generate isolated swarm CSSM branches, and use council outcomes to synthesize evolved canonical state versions.
protocol_implications: Architecture evolution becomes an explicit workflow with replayable decisions, not just a narrative instruction set.
security_implications: Recovery and audit improve because all critical decisions become append-only telemetry events.
title: Event-Sourced Recursive Evolution Control Plane
